prior.claim_text: Analytical inference: The trilogue has now concluded at political level (Commission welcomes agreement per the March 2026 update). Final text and entry into force timeline require monitoring. Workspaces holding AEO status should note that the Council position preserved AEO status alongside Trust & Check, rather than abolishing it — a significant reversal from the original Commission proposal.
prior.source_span: The abolition of AEO status for those economic operators who do not fulfil the requirements of Trust & Check status, as envisaged in the original Commission proposal, has not been adopted by the Council.

prior.claim_text: Analytical inference: For the workspace handling air freight for live events, fine art, and high-value automotive cargo — categories where commodity descriptions on commercial invoices may be abbreviated or use internal trade terminology — tighter automated screening from September 2026 creates an elevated risk of ENS rejection at the pre-loading phase, which could cause cargo to miss aircraft departures.
prior.source_span: By September 2026, the EU's automated risk analysis systems will apply tighter quality thresholds to every ENS submission. Data that has historically passed with minimal scrutiny, vague commodity descriptions, incomplete shipper addresses on commercial invoices, or borderline HS6 code accuracy, will increasingly trigger automated holds and requests for additional information.

prior.claim_text: It is now mandatory for anyone shipping goods into or transitioning through the EU, Norway, Switzerland and Northern Ireland using air, maritime and inland waterways, road and rail transportation modes to provide a six-digit Harmonized System (HS) code for each commodity line listed in the commercial invoice and or detailed goods descriptions for the shipments prior to arrival of goods. Where more than one HS code is present, the net weight of each is also required. The Economic Operators Registration and Identification Number (EORI) for the shipment recipient consignee is also mandatory.
prior.source_span: It is now mandatory for anyone shipping goods into or transitioning through the EU, Norway, Switzerland and Northern Ireland using air, maritime and inland waterways, road and rail transportation modes to provide a six-digit Harmonized System (HS) code for each commodity line listed in the commercial invoice and or detailed goods descriptions for the shipments prior to arrival of goods. Where more than one HS code is present, the net weight of each is also required. The Economic Operators Registration and Identification Number (EORI) for the shipment recipient consignee is also mandatory.

prior.claim_text: An Economic Operator Registration and Identification (EORI) number is mandatory to connect to ICS2. prior.source_span: An Economic Operator Registration and Identification (EORI) number is mandatory to connect to ICS2. prior.claim_text: Under the SAF revenue certainty mechanism, SAF producers will enter into a private law contract with a government-backed counterparty. These contracts will set a strike price for SAF: if producers sell their SAF for below the strike price, the counterparty makes payments of the difference; if the SAF is sold for above the strike price, the producer makes payments of the difference to the counterparty.
prior.source_span: Under the SAF revenue certainty mechanism, SAF producers will enter into a private law contract with a government-backed counterparty. These contracts will set a strike price for SAF: if producers sell their SAF for below the strike price, the counterparty makes payments of the difference; if the SAF is sold for above the strike price, the producer makes payments of the difference to the counterparty

prior.claim_text: Default emission values are introduced for countries lacking reliable data. These will be set at the highest emission intensity observed among countries with reliable data for the respective product type or based on the applicable region-specific values. For aluminium, steel, and iron, embedded emissions from precursors are counted by default, while emissions from finishing processes are no longer counted, in alignment with EU ETS rules.
prior.source_span: Default emission values are introduced for countries lacking reliable data. These will be set at the highest emission intensity observed among countries with reliable data for the respective product type or based on the applicable region-specific values. prior.claim_text: The full recovery and recycled content target trajectory is: 32% recovery / 22% recycled content in 2026; 37% / 30% by 2030; 50% / 40% by 2040.
prior.source_span: The law sets national recovery targets of 32% for 2026, 37% by 2030, and 50% by 2040. For every 5% of returnable packaging collected, the recovery target for manufacturers and importers will be reduced by 1%, up to a limit of 50% of the target. At the same time, companies will be held to recycled content targets of 22% by 2026, 30% by 2030, and 40% by 2040.
